examples.title: Earned Value Management Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate EVM metrics and determine project health status
examples.code:
BEGIN
 INPUT: PV, EV, AC, BAC, threshold
 
 // Calculate Variances
 CV = EV - AC
 SV = EV - PV
 
 // Calculate Performance Indices
 IF AC > 0 THEN
 CPI = EV / AC
 ELSE
 CPI = 0
 ENDIF
 
 IF PV > 0 THEN
 SPI = EV / PV
 ELSE
 SPI = 0
 ENDIF
 
 // Calculate Forecasts
 IF CPI > 0 THEN
 EAC = BAC / CPI
 ELSE
 EAC = BAC + AC
 ENDIF
 
 ETC = EAC - AC
 VAC = BAC - EAC
 
 // Determine Health Status
 IF CPI >= 1.0 AND SPI >= 1.0 THEN
 Status = "GREEN - On Track"
 ELSE IF CPI >= 0.8 AND SPI >= 0.8 THEN
 Status = "YELLOW - At Risk"
 ELSE
 Status = "RED - Critical Attention Required"
 ENDIF
 
 OUTPUT: CV, SV, CPI, SPI, EAC, ETC, VAC, Status
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode calculates all fundamental EVM metrics including variances, performance indices, and forecasts. It uses conditional logic to determine project health status based on industry-standard thresholds (CPI/SPI >= 1.0 = green, >= 0.8 = yellow, otherwise red). The algorithm enables project managers to quickly assess project health and take corrective action.

examples.title: Weighted Scoring Model for Project Evaluation
examples.description: Algorithm to evaluate and rank multiple projects using weighted criteria
examples.code:
BEGIN
 INPUT: Projects[], Criteria[], Weights[], Scores[][]
 
 // Validate inputs
 totalWeight = SUM(Weights)
 IF totalWeight != 1.0 THEN
 PRINT "Warning: Weights should sum to 1.0"
 // Normalize weights
 FOR i = 0 TO Criteria.length-1 DO
 Weights[i] = Weights[i] / totalWeight
 ENDFOR
 ENDIF
 
 // Calculate weighted scores for each project
 FOR p = 0 TO Projects.length-1 DO
 weightedScore = 0
 FOR c = 0 TO Criteria.length-1 DO
 weightedScore = weightedScore + (Weights[c] * Scores[p][c])
 ENDFOR
 ProjectScores[p] = weightedScore
 ENDFOR
 
 // Rank projects
 SortedProjects = SORT(ProjectScores, DESCENDING)
 
 // Output results
 FOR p = 0 TO Projects.length-1 DO
 PRINT Projects[p].name, ": ", ProjectScores[p]
 ENDFOR
 
 RETURN SortedProjects[0] // Best project
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm implements a multi-criteria decision analysis using weighted scoring. It normalizes weights if they don't sum to 1.0, calculates weighted scores for each project by multiplying each criterion score by its weight, and ranks projects in descending order. The highest-scoring project is recommended for selection.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × m + n log n) where n=projects, m=criteria

examples.title: Project Health Check Monitoring System
examples.description: Continuous monitoring system that tracks project health indicators and alerts stakeholders
examples.code:
BEGIN
 INPUT: currentCost, budgetedCost, earnedValue, plannedValue
 
 // Calculate key metrics
 costVariancePercent = ((currentCost - budgetedCost) / budgetedCost) * 100
 scheduleVariancePercent = ((plannedValue - earnedValue) / plannedValue) * 100
 
 // Determine individual status
 costStatus = GET_STATUS(costVariancePercent)
 scheduleStatus = GET_STATUS(scheduleVariancePercent)
 qualityStatus = GET_QUALITY_STATUS()
 scopeStatus = GET_SCOPE_STATUS()
 
 // Determine overall health
 statuses = [costStatus, scheduleStatus, qualityStatus, scopeStatus]
 IF statuses CONTAINS "RED" THEN
 overallHealth = "RED"
 ELSE IF statuses CONTAINS "YELLOW" THEN
 overallHealth = "YELLOW"
 ELSE
 overallHealth = "GREEN"
 ENDIF
 
 // Generate alerts
 IF overallHealth != "GREEN" THEN
 ALERT_STAKEHOLDERS(overallHealth, statuses)
 RECOMMEND_CORRECTIVE_ACTIONS(statuses)
 ENDIF
 
 OUTPUT: overallHealth, costStatus, scheduleStatus, qualityStatus, scopeStatus
END

FUNCTION GET_STATUS(variancePercent)
 IF variancePercent <= 10 THEN
 RETURN "GREEN"
 ELSE IF variancePercent <= 25 THEN
 RETURN "YELLOW"
 ELSE
 RETURN "RED"
 ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode implements a traffic light health monitoring system. It calculates variance percentages for cost and schedule, maps them to traffic light indicators using configurable thresholds, determines overall project health based on the worst indicator, and triggers alerts with corrective action recommendations when health deteriorates.
